user_prompt: see the git diff about `lib\arduino-liblame\src\liblame\lame.h`, how is this change I just made by hand?
agent_reply: Your hand change is mechanically valid: it makes callers that do
select `vbr_off`, so that pushes the wrapper toward CBR instead of VBR.

1. I would not love changing `vbr_default` in [lame.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/arduino-liblame/src/liblame/lame.h:67). That is a global semantic change to the library API. Cleaner would be changing the wrapper/config path to call `lame_set_VBR(lame, vbr_off)` explicitly.

2. This does not set the bitrate. The wrapper currently sets VBR mode, channels, sample rate, and quality, but not `lame_set_brate(...)`. With 16 kHz stereo, LAME will pick a CBR bitrate from its defaults, likely around 48 kbps, not necessarily the value you want.

One more practical gotcha: the last build appeared to be using `.pio/libdeps/thefly_sec1/libLAME` from the GitHub dependency, not necessarily your local `lib/arduino-liblame` copy. That dependency copy still has `vbr_default = vbr_mtrh`, so your hand edit may not affect firmware unless PlatformIO is actually resolving the vendored library.
So: right idea for “force CBR,” but I’d do it explicitly in the wrapper and set an explicit bitrate too.
